rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Creator name in body prose above the maintainer cut. The framed-memo exception requires the opening to frame this as a historical memo; no such frame exists here. Remove the signature or move below <!-- maintainer -->.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 44:The trainer reads the room and picks the form (pair exchange, whole-room round, quiet write-then-share, or skip the round when energy is spent).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Trainer-facing room-management instruction in body prose above the maintainer cut. Move the trainer-directive sentence to the maintainer block; the student-facing sentence (the italic closer + self-study variant) can stay.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Mild reassurance shape — peer voice doesn't apologise for the self-study variant's brevity. Cut the clause; let the marker statement stand on its own.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 file — engineer audience. Bootstrap-specific rule does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
Golden path only in the body.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 14:**Compound by subtraction** (Evaluate) — cut one rule the two-run diagnosis killed from `./CLAUDE.local.md`; | 16:evals as the broader family — verifier in your repo, judge in your test, gate in your CI — same function, different surfaces.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Two em-dashes in What You'll Learn body (lines 14 and 16). Split at the em-dash into two short sentences or rewrite with a colon.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Non-canonical section header describing an action phase; 'Human close' is a noun phrase, not a command verb. Consider 'Close the loop' or similar.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 56:On Monday, pick a task your team is sitting on
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Day-of-week anchor breaks when training runs on any cadence. Replace with 'After this module' or 'In your next slot' to be cadence-agnostic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
No meta-ask when the student has already decided.
Cut NVA: true but not moving the student forward.
No overclaim of student verification or coverage
No narrating the student's past or present state
